title: Loop Orchestration: Outcome Determination + run_finished
summary: Full run orchestration in golemic run --issue. Executes the sequence: collision check (I1.3) → dev worktree (I1.4) → dev prompt+agent (I1.5/I1.6) → read event log for pr_opened → reviewer worktree (I1.8) → reviewer prompt+agent (I1.5/I1.6) → dirty check (I1.8) → read event log for review_submitted → determine outcome (success, dev_failed, review_failed, timeout, aborted) → write run_finished → cleanup on success. Runner exit 0 only on success; otherwise != 0 with run directory path.

stakeholder_intent.goal: Run one complete round of dev→PR→review and get a deterministic outcome
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Fully automated one-round loop: the runner drives the entire dev→review pipeline, records every step in the event log, and exits with a clear outcome. The maintainer (or future loop) knows exactly what happened.
stakeholder_intent.trigger: golemic run --issue N after collision check passes (I1.3)
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: A dev commits, pushes, opens a PR; a reviewer reviews it; the outcome is success; worktrees are cleaned up; exit code 0

in_scope: Full orchestration in run --issue: I1.3 → I1.4 → I1.5/I1.6 (Dev) → read pr_opened → I1.8 → I1.5/I1.6 (Reviewer) → dirty check → read review_submitted → outcome determination
Outcome enum: success, dev_failed, review_failed, timeout, aborted
run_finished event with outcome as last event in all paths
Runner exit 0 only on success; exit != 0 with message and runs/<runId>/ path otherwise
Outcome rules: missing pr_opened → dev_failed; missing review_submitted or dirty → review_failed; role timeout → timeout; collision → aborted; otherwise success
out_of_scope: Escalation via PR comment — planned in Iteration 2
Recovery from event log — planned in a subsequent iteration

preconditions: Event log (I1.1) is available for reading and writing

business_rules.rule: run_finished is always the last event in the log, even in error paths
business_rules.outcome: run_finished event is written after all other events, with the outcome field set to the final outcome

business_rules.rule: If pr_opened event is missing or malformed after the dev run, outcome is dev_failed
business_rules.outcome: Read event log for pr_opened; if not found or malformed → dev_failed, skip reviewer

business_rules.rule: If review_submitted event is missing or malformed after the reviewer run, or dirty check fails, outcome is review_failed
business_rules.outcome: Read event log for review_submitted; if not found or malformed, or dirty check was dirty → review_failed

business_rules.rule: If the dev agent times out, outcome is timeout
business_rules.outcome: timeout outcome; skip reviewer; write run_finished with timeout

business_rules.rule: If the reviewer agent times out, outcome is timeout
business_rules.outcome: timeout outcome; write run_finished with timeout

business_rules.rule: Cleanup (worktree removal) is called only for outcome success
business_rules.outcome: If outcome is success, call worktree cleanup for both dev and reviewer worktrees. Otherwise, leave worktrees in place for debugging.

business_rules.rule: Runner exit code is 0 only for outcome success; all other outcomes exit != 0
business_rules.outcome: Exit 0 for success; exit != 0 for all other outcomes, with a message and the path to runs/<runId>/

business_rules.rule: If the collision check aborts (I1.3), outcome is aborted and run_finished is written
business_rules.outcome: run_finished with outcome aborted is written to the event log; exit != 0

outputs.body: run_finished event with outcome success, worktrees cleaned up
run_finished event with outcome, runs/<runId>/ path printed, worktrees left in place

process_steps.name: Collision check and run start
process_steps.action: Resolve host repo, load config/credentials, create runId, write run_started, load issue, check collisions
process_steps.state_after: run_started written, no collision, issue loaded
process_steps.failure_behavior: On collision: write run_finished with outcome aborted, exit != 0

process_steps.name: Create dev worktree
process_steps.action: Create dev worktree from origin/main, set git config, write worktree_created event
process_steps.failure_behavior: Error: dev_failed, write run_finished, skip to cleanup

process_steps.name: Run dev agent
process_steps.action: Render dev prompt (I1.5), execute dev agent (I1.6) with timeout, capture transcript
process_steps.state_before: Dev worktree exists, no agent output
process_steps.state_after: Dev agent finished (or timed out), transcript files written
process_steps.outcome: Determine next step based on pr_opened event presence
process_steps.failure_behavior: Timeout → outcome timeout, write run_finished, skip to exit. Event missing → dev_failed.

process_steps.name: Create reviewer worktree
process_steps.trigger: pr_opened event exists and is valid
process_steps.action: Create reviewer worktree from origin/golemic/issue-<N>, set git config, write worktree_created event (role: reviewer)
process_steps.failure_behavior: Error: review_failed, write run_finished, skip to exit

process_steps.name: Run reviewer agent
process_steps.action: Render reviewer prompt (I1.5), execute reviewer agent (I1.6) with timeout, capture transcript
process_steps.state_before: Reviewer worktree exists, no agent output
process_steps.state_after: Reviewer agent finished (or timed out), transcript files written
process_steps.outcome: Determine outcome based on review_submitted event and dirty check
process_steps.failure_behavior: Timeout → outcome timeout. Event missing or dirty → review_failed.

process_steps.name: Determine outcome and clean up
process_steps.trigger: All steps completed or any failure occurred
process_steps.action: Write run_finished with final outcome, cleanup worktrees on success, print run path on failure
process_steps.state_before: All events written up to the last step
process_steps.state_after: run_finished written, worktrees cleaned up (if success) or left in place (if failure)

authorization_rules: Only the runner process may invoke the full orchestration (internal package, CLI entry point)
audit_requirements: run_finished event records the final outcome
Full event log and transcripts are available under runs/<runId>/

architecture_constraints: run_finished must always be the last event
allowed_changes: Extend the runner orchestration to include all steps
forbidden_changes: Do not skip cleanup on success
Do not write run_finished before the final outcome is determined
Do not exit 0 on failure outcomes
implementation_context.migration_strategy: Extension of existing runner package; no migration needed
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Single-threaded; each step runs sequentially. The event log provides the authoritative record of what happened.

acceptance_scenarios.title: Happy path: dev → PR → review → success
given: Reviewer agent writes review_submitted event with valid verdict
then: run_finished event is written with outcome success
Dev and reviewer worktrees are cleaned up

acceptance_scenarios.title: Missing pr_opened → dev_failed
given: Dev agent does NOT write pr_opened event
then: run_finished event is written with outcome dev_failed

acceptance_scenarios.title: Missing review_submitted → review_failed
given: Reviewer agent does NOT write review_submitted event
then: run_finished event is written with outcome review_failed

acceptance_scenarios.title: Dev agent timeout → timeout outcome
given: Dev agent is configured with a very short timeout
Fake agent does not complete in time
then: run_finished event is written with outcome timeout

acceptance_scenarios.title: Collision → aborted outcome
given: Worktree already exists for the issue
then: run_finished event is written with outcome aborted

acceptance_scenarios.title: run_finished is always the last event
given: Any outcome path (success, failure, timeout, aborted)
when: golemic run --issue 42 is invoked and terminates
then: The last event in the event log is always run_finished
No event is written after run_finished

acceptance_scenarios.title: Reviewer timeout is handled correctly
given: Reviewer agent is configured with a very short timeout
then: run_finished event is written with outcome timeout

test_cases: Happy path: success outcome with cleanup
run_finished is always the last event
Exit code is 0 only for success
Worktrees cleaned up only on success
Error message includes runs/<runId>/ path on failure
non_functional_requirements: All external commands go through injectable interfaces (Executor via preflight package)
Table-driven tests cover all outcome paths
definition_of_done: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test -count=1 ./... is green
All outcomes (success, dev_failed, review_failed, timeout, aborted) are implemented
run_finished is always the last event in the log

decision_log.decision: Outcome is determined by event presence, dirty check, and timeout, not by agent exit code
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'Das Event ist die Wahrheit, der Exit-Code nur Log-Information' and §2.11

decision_log.decision: Worktrees are cleaned up only on success outcome; left in place for debugging on failure
decision_log.rationale: Specified in §2.11: 'Worktrees bleiben bei Fehlern stehen (Debugging); Cleanup nur bei success'

decision_log.decision: Exit 0 only for success; all other outcomes exit != 0 with run directory path
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'Runner-Exit 0 nur bei success, sonst ≠ 0 mit Meldung + Pfad zu runs/<runId>/'

decision_log.decision: Loop decisions are based on events in the log, not on agent exit codes or stdout parsing
decision_log.rationale: Specified in §2.6: 'Das Event-Log ist die interne Wahrheit' and §2.2: 'nur Exit-Code und Timeout auswerten'

decision_log.decision: The orchestration uses the Executor interface for all external commands (gh, git, pi)
decision_log.rationale: internal/preflight defines the injectable Executor interface; all external commands in the loop go through it

decision_log.decision: The run subcommand follows the existing dispatch pattern in main.go
decision_log.rationale: main.go dispatches subcommands by name; run will get a dedicated handler similar to preflight

decision_log.decision: Timeout and model are read from the Config struct
decision_log.rationale: Config.TimeoutMinutes and Config.Models are used for timeout enforcement and model selection in agent execution

codebase_evidence.finding: Executor interface with Run and RunWithEnv methods exists; all external commands in the orchestration go through this interface
Subcommand dispatch pattern with preflight as the only implemented subcommand; run is stubbed with 'not implemented'
Config struct with TimeoutMinutes (default 30) and Models fields exists; orchestration reads these for timeout and model selection
